findings.title: Prompt Injection via f-string Interpolation
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into a prompt string with an f-string. Attackers can craft malicious input to override system instructions, extract data, or hijack model behavior.
findings.recommendation: Use ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages() with named slots instead of f-strings. Validate/sanitize user input before including in any prompt.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ VULNERABILITY 3: PII logged before LLM call (LLM06 - MEDIUM)
    logger.info(f"Calling LLM with prompt: {prompt}")

    response = llm.invoke(prompt)

findings.title: Prompt Injection via f-string Interpolation
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into a prompt string with an f-string. Attackers can craft malicious input to override system instructions, extract data, or hijack model behavior.
findings.recommendation: Use ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages() with named slots instead of f-strings. Validate/sanitize user input before including in any prompt.
findings.code_snippet:
# Ask LLM to generate SQL query
    sql_prompt = f"Generate a SQL query to: {user_input}"
    llm_generated_sql = llm.invoke(sql_prompt).content

findings.title: Prompt Injection via f-string Interpolation
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into a prompt string with an f-string. Attackers can craft malicious input to override system instructions, extract data, or hijack model behavior.
findings.recommendation: Use ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages() with named slots instead of f-strings. Validate/sanitize user input before including in any prompt.
findings.code_snippet:
from flask import render_template_string

    response = llm.invoke(f"Format this message as HTML: {user_message}")
    llm_response = response.content

findings.title: Direct LLM Call with f-string Argument
findings.description: The LLM is invoked with an f-string argument. If the interpolated variable is user-controlled this is a direct prompt injection vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Refactor to use PromptTemplate and pass user data as named variables, not embedded strings.
findings.code_snippet:
from flask import render_template_string

    response = llm.invoke(f"Format this message as HTML: {user_message}")
    llm_response = response.content

findings.title: XSS: LLM Output Rendered as Raw HTML
findings.description: LLM-generated content is rendered as raw HTML. An attacker who can influence the model output (e.g., via indirect prompt injection) can inject malicious scripts.
findings.recommendation: Use HTML templating with auto-escaping (Jinja2 autoescape=True). Never pass LLM output to render_template_string() or Markup().
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ VULNERABILITY 7: LLM output rendered as raw HTML (XSS)
    return render_template_string(llm_response)

findings.title: Excessive Agency: Agent Has Shell Execution Access
findings.description: A LangChain ShellTool is registered to the agent. This gives the LLM the ability to execute any shell command. If compromised via prompt injection, an attacker can run arbitrary OS commands.
findings.recommendation: Replace ShellTool with specific, allowlisted functions. Apply the principle of least privilege. Add human-in-the-loop approval for any destructive operations.
findings.code_snippet:
3. [LLM06-HIGH]     PII (user email + name) sent to LLM
4. [LLM02-CRITICAL] LLM output executed with eval()
5. [LLM08-CRITICAL] Agent with ShellTool (unrestricted shell access)
6. [LLM06-MEDIUM]   LLM prompt/response logged to console
"""

findings.title: Excessive Agency: Agent Has Shell Execution Access
findings.description: A LangChain ShellTool is registered to the agent. This gives the LLM the ability to execute any shell command. If compromised via prompt injection, an attacker can run arbitrary OS commands.
findings.recommendation: Replace ShellTool with specific, allowlisted functions. Apply the principle of least privilege. Add human-in-the-loop approval for any destructive operations.
findings.code_snippet:
# ShellTool gives the LLM access to execute ANY shell command
    tools = [ShellTool()]  # CRITICAL: rm -rf / anyone?

    agent = initialize_agent(

findings.title: Hardcoded OpenAI API Key
findings.description: An OpenAI API key (sk-...) is hardcoded in the source code. Keys in source code are often accidentally committed to version control and exposed publicly.
findings.recommendation: Store API keys in environment variables or a secrets manager. Rotate any exposed key immediately.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ VULNERABILITY 1: Hardcoded API key (LLM06 - CRITICAL)
OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-abc123realapikeyexposedingithub"
os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"] = OPENAI_API_KEY

findings.title: Prompt Injection via TypeScript Template Literal
findings.description: User input is interpolated directly into a template literal used in an LLM API call.
findings.recommendation: Use structured message arrays. Pass user content as a separate message object, not as part of a template literal.

findings.title: Prompt Injection via TypeScript Template Literal
findings.description: User input is interpolated directly into a template literal used in an LLM API call.
findings.recommendation: Use structured message arrays. Pass user content as a separate message object, not as part of a template literal.

findings.title: Prompt Injection via TypeScript Template Literal
findings.description: User input is interpolated directly into a template literal used in an LLM API call.
findings.recommendation: Use structured message arrays. Pass user content as a separate message object, not as part of a template literal.

findings.title: XSS: LLM Output Set as innerHTML
findings.description: LLM output is assigned to innerHTML without sanitization. This enables XSS if the model output contains malicious script tags.
findings.recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML. If HTML rendering is needed, use a sanitizer like DOMPurify.

findings.title: RCE: LLM Output Passed to eval()/Function()
findings.description: LLM output is executed as JavaScript via eval() or Function(). This is a critical code execution vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Never execute LLM output as code. Use structured output formats (JSON) and process them safely.

findings.title: Hardcoded OpenAI Project API Key (TypeScript)
findings.description: An OpenAI Project API key is hardcoded in TypeScript source.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables. Never include secrets in client-side bundles.

findings.title: Hardcoded Anthropic API Key
findings.description: An Anthropic API key (sk-ant-...) is hardcoded in the source code. This credential can be extracted from the repository and used for unauthorized LLM access.
findings.recommendation: Use os.environ['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] or python-dotenv. Rotate exposed keys immediately.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ Hardcoded Anthropic API key (SEC-ANTHROPIC - CRITICAL)
client = Anthropic(api_key="sk-ant-api03-realexposedkey12345678")

# Global vector store (shared across all users — no tenant isolation)

findings.title: Brazilian CPF Number Hardcoded in Source
findings.description: A Brazilian CPF tax number pattern was found hardcoded in source code. This is a LGPD-sensitive PII that must not appear in code or be sent to external LLM APIs.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded CPF values. Mask PII before sending to external LLMs. Review LGPD compliance for your data processing pipeline.
findings.code_snippet:
prompt = f"""
    You are a helpful assistant for {user_name} ({user_email}).
    User CPF: 123.456.789-00
    
    Answer the following question: {user_input}

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key or Token
findings.description: A generic API key or token appears to be hardcoded in source code.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or a secrets manager. Never commit credentials to version control.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ VULNERABILITY 1: Hardcoded API key (LLM06 - CRITICAL)
OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-abc123realapikeyexposedingithub"
os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"] = OPENAI_API_KEY

findings.title: Excessive Agency: Agent Has Filesystem Write/Delete Access
findings.description: A file manipulation tool is registered to the agent. The LLM can write, move, or delete arbitrary files, potentially destroying data or writing malicious content.
findings.recommendation: Use read-only file tools. For write operations, require explicit human approval and restrict to a sandboxed directory.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key or Token

findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or a secrets manager. Never commit credentials to version control.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ Hardcoded Anthropic API key (SEC-ANTHROPIC - CRITICAL)
client = Anthropic(api_key="sk-ant-api03-realexposedkey12345678")

# Global vector store (shared across all users — no tenant isolation)

findings.title: RAG: No Access Control on Document Retrieval
findings.description: The RAG retriever is called with raw user input without apparent access control. Any user may retrieve documents they are not authorized to see.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-user document filtering using metadata. Check user permissions before including retrieved documents in context.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ RAG retrieval with raw user input (no sanitization, no access control)
    retrieved_docs = retriever.get_relevant_documents(user_input)

    # ❌ Logging retrieved documents (may contain confidential data)

findings.title: Sensitive Data Logged: LLM Prompt or Response
findings.description: Full LLM prompts or responses are being written to logs. These may contain PII, credentials, or business-sensitive data persisted without access controls.
findings.recommendation: Log only metadata (latency, token count, model name, session ID). Implement PII scrubbing if content logging is required.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ VULNERABILITY 3: PII logged before LLM call (LLM06 - MEDIUM)
    logger.info(f"Calling LLM with prompt: {prompt}")

    response = llm.invoke(prompt)

findings.description: Full LLM prompts or responses are being written to logs. These may contain PII, credentials, or business-sensitive data persisted without access controls.
findings.recommendation: Log only metadata (latency, token count, model name, session ID). Implement PII scrubbing if content logging is required.
findings.code_snippet:
# ❌ VULNERABILITY 5: Response logged with full content (LLM06 - MEDIUM)
    logger.debug(f"LLM response: {result}")

    return result

findings.title: Excessive Agency: Agent Configured with High max_iterations
findings.description: The agent is configured with a very high max_iterations value (50+). This increases the risk of runaway agents consuming resources or taking unintended actions.
findings.recommendation: Set max_iterations to 5-10 for most use cases. Add timeouts and cost limits.
findings.code_snippet:
agent=AgentType.ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION,
        verbose=False,   # ❌ No audit trail
        max_iterations=100,  # ❌ Runaway agent risk
    )
    return agent
